rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Reconsideration Of Order To Gregory Crowe To Deposit 3/4 Of The Hodges Sales Proceeds In A Blocked Account (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2] Attorney Madden's declaration submitted in support of this motion complies with Code of Civil Procedure section 2015.5. Madden's paragraph regarding Crowe's personal inability to comply with the court's direction to place ¾ of the Hodges Aly sale proceeds into a blocked account as ordered is not based on Madden's personal knowledge, however, but instead on what Crowe has told him: "I have been informed by my client that neither he nor the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust has that amount of money to deposit - in fact, he has very little money assets at all. Therefore, it is literally impossible for him to comply with the order." To be admissible as evidence, a declaration must satisfy the same criteria as oral testimony, i.e., it must state facts showing that the declarant has personal knowledge of the facts stated and could competently testify to these facts. (See Evid. Code section 702). Any declaration is, by definition, hearsay; Code of Civil Procedure section 2009 provides a specific hearsay exception for declarations, exempting the first "layer" of hearsay, the presentation of an out-of-court statement to the court. (McDonald v. Sup. Crt. (1994) 22 Cal. App. 4th 364, 370.) Madden's statement that is client told him something and the contents of what he was told is both within Madden's personal knowledge of what he was told, and is admissible under the hearsay exception provided by section 2009 as a presentation of an out-of-court statement to the court. The veracity of Crowe's out-of-court statement and Madden's conclusions based on that statement - i.e. the rest of paragraph 3 - are not admissible, and carry no evidentiary weight. Even if these evidentiary issues in the declarations submitted in support of the motion did not exist, the motion before the court would still fail to show how Crowe's knowledge of his current allegedly dire financial situation - apparently known to Crowe but unknown to his attorney as of the November 14, 2022 hearing and now presented to the court for the first time - has any bearing on the legal correctness of the court's order. The court sees no legal reason to reconsider its November 14, 2022 order that disputed funds be deposited into a blocked account. "My client already spent the money" - even if factually true and only recently discovered by counsel - does not affect the legal basis on which the order was entered. The motion presents no convincing legal argument to the contrary. The court the orders, on its own motion, a full accounting of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust, to include (1) closing documents from the Hodges Aly sale, and (2) all bank records showing all money flowing in and out of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust. The full accounting, with documents and records, must be filed within 30 days of entry of this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Adjudication On Fifth Claim And The Sixth Claim That Maureen Crowes July 16, 2016 Hodges Aly Deeds Are Certain, Absolute, And Effective, And Against The Claim And Defense Of Gregory Crowe That Maureen Made Her Deeds Conditioned Or Conditional, I.E., Allegedly Being On Hold And Not Finalized Unless And Until She Had Substantive
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] As is often the case in disputes over the validity and effect of competing testamentary instruments, the person whose intentions and state of mind at a particular period of time are the core of the legal and factual disputes at issue - Maureen Crowe - has passed away. She cannot inform us of her intent, either in signing the 2016 deeds or signing the subsequent financing and sale documents or any other action or inaction she took with respect to these trusts and properties. The trier of fact will be required to make factual and legal determinations about her intent based on the disputed significance of Decedent's actions as an individual and as the settlor and trustee of her two trusts. After making credibility determinations, resolving conflicts and drawing reasonable inferences from the evidence currently before the court on this motion, a reasonable trier of fact could find that the challenged 2016 deeds were effective upon execution; that they were "delivered" and "accepted" by Decedent in her various individual and fiduciary capacities at the time she executed them; that the statutory presumption a deed is "delivered" on the date of execution has not been overcome; and thereafter find that title on the Hodges Aly and Greenwood Street properties ultimately transferred from the 2004 Trust, to the 2016 Trust. In order to reach this conclusion, however, the reasonable trier of fact would also need make factual findings. The trier of fact would have to consider the evidence offered regarding Decedent's subsequent actions in her purported capacity of Trustee of the 2004 Trust, as well as her statements to Petitioner regarding the 2016 Trust as "on hold" and "not finalized" in the period between its execution and Decedent's death in 2019, and the reasonable inferences that might be drawn therefrom. In sum, a decision crediting the effectiveness of the 2016 deeds would require factual findings regarding the assertions by Petitioner about the significance of Decedent's actions and inactions as described. The court, on a motion for summary adjudication, cannot convert these disputed facts into matters of law. It does not matter that the moving party finds the disputed assertions specious or incredible - freewheeling, even. A finder of fact will have to make a determination as to the disputed material facts presented by the opposing parties before this dispute can be resolved. That factual determination is outside the limited statutory power of a motion for summary adjudication. The existence of triable issues of material fact in the record preclude summary adjudication. To rule otherwise on the record before the court on this motion would very likely be deemed an abuse of discretion, and would inject reversable error into these proceedings. The Court declines to take this step. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Fifth Claim And The Sixth Claim That Maureen Crowes July 16, 2016 Hodges Aly Deeds Are Certain, Absolute, And Effective, And Against The Claim And Defense Of Gregory Crowe That Maureen Made Her Deeds Conditioned Or Conditional, I.E., Allegedly Being On Hold And Not Finalized Unless And Until She Had Substantive Changes Made
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Robert Callahan and Patricia Callahan, in her personal capacity and as Executor of the Estate of Edward Callahan, filed Petition for Removal, Appointment, or Confirmation of Trustee; for Breach of Fiduciary Duty and Damages; to Compel Trust Accounting, on October 16, 2020, and an Amended Petition on May 14, 2021. Gregory Crowe, as Beneficiary and Trustee of the related Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust, filed an Answer to the Amended Petition on July 8, 2021. In the related case, In the Matter of the Lia Fail 2004 Trust (PTR-21-304179), Gregory Crowe, as Beneficiary and Trustee of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust, filed Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property, on February 4, 2021. Robert Callahan and Patricia Callahan, in her personal capacity and as Executor of the Estate of Edward Callahan, filed Response to Gregory Crowe's Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property, on April 15, 2021. Gregory Crowe's Motion for Summary Judgment in the related matter was heard and denied on November 14, 2022. Now before the court is Patricia Callahan, in her personal capacity and as Executor of the Estate of Edward Callahan, and Robert Callahan's Motion for Summary Adjudication on the Fifth Claim and the Sixth Claim That Maureen Crowe's July 16, 2016 Hodges Aly Deeds Are Certain, Absolute and Effective, and Against the Claim and Defense of Gregory Crowe That Maureen Made Her Deeds Conditioned or Conditional, i.e., Allegedly Being "On Hold" and "Not Finalized Unless and Until She Had Substantive Changes Made." The court GRANTS opposing party Gregory Crowe's request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Patricia Callahan, in her personal capacity and as Executor of the Estate of Edward Callahan, and Robert Callahan's Motion for Summary Adjudication on the Fifth Claim and the Sixth Claim That Maureen Crowe's July 16, 2016 Hodges Aly Deeds Are Certain, Absolute and Effective, and Against the Claim and Defense of Gregory Crowe That Maureen Made Her Deeds Conditioned or Conditional, i.e., Allegedly Being "On Hold" and "Not Finalized Unless and Until She Had Substantive Changes Made" is DENIED. Summary adjudication must completely dispose of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, or an issue of duty. (Code of Civ. Proc. section 437c.) In order to grant a motion for summary adjudication, the court must find that there is no triable issue of material fact as to a particular claim or defense. Inferences from the evidence must favor the party opposing the motion; one disputed material fact is all that is necessary to deny the motion. It is not disputed that Decedent Maureen Crowe executed both the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust and the Maureen E. Crowe Trust, with herself as settlor and initial trustee. (Response to Crowe's Sep. Stmnt. at pars. 1, 2, 5, 6, 7.) It is not disputed that Decedent executed quitclaim deeds in July 2016 that ultimately quitclaimed the Hodges Aly property, the Greenwood Street property, and the 510 Elm Street property to the 2016 Trust. (Response to Crowe's Sep. Stmnt. at pars. 1, 2, 6, 8). It is not disputed that Maureen Crowe thereafter took actions in connection with the Hodges Aly property, and the Greenwood Street property (Response to Sep. Stmnt. at pars 11, 12, 13, 14, 16). The parties do dispute the purported legal capacity in which Maureen took these actions, however (Response to Sep. Stmnt. at pars 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16). [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order To Gregory Crowe To Deposit ¾ Of The Hodges Sales Proceeds In A Blocked Account
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Motion for Reconsideration of Order to Gregory Crowe to Deposit ¾ of the Hodges Aly Sales Proceeds in a Blocked Account, brought by Gregory J. Crowe, as Beneficiary and Trustee of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust, pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. section 1008. At the November 14, 2022 hearing in this matter, as reflected in the Minutes, the court instructed attorney Ross McLauran Madden, counsel for Gregory J. Crowe, "that 3/4 of the net proceeds from the sale [of Hodges Aly] must be deposited and he must file an ex parte application for a court order to deposit those funds in a blocked account and to file the closing statement." The Motion for Reconsideration of Order to Gregory Crowe to Deposit ¾ of the Hodges Aly Sales Proceeds in a Blocked Account, brought by Gregory J. Crowe, as Beneficiary and Trustee of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust is DENIED. Procedurally, the court finds this motion is not untimely under Code of Civ. Proc. section 1008. The statutory 10-day period in which to bring a motion for reconsideration of an order is calculated from "within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order." Here, there was no "service [] of written notice of entry of the order." Substantively, the motion does present to the court for the first time that Crowe no longer possesses the proceeds from the Hodges Aly property sale he completed in his purported capacity as successor trustee of the related Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust. The rightful possession and distribution of these funds has been an overt part of the legal dispute in both this and the related case, In the Matter of the Lia Fail 2004 Trust (PTR-21-304179), since the cases' inceptions in 2021. However, the declaration of Gregory Crowe submitted in support of this new assertion is unverified insofar as it does not comply with the statutory requirements of a declaration under penalty of perjury and thus presents no evidentiary value to the court in considering this motion. (Code of Civ. Proc. section 2015.5; see, e.g., Bombardier Recreational Products, Inc. v. Dow Chemical Canada ULC (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 591, 605.) [Part 1 of 2]
